content.content:
1	# Story 3.3: Evidence Report on Threshold Fallback
2	
3	Status: ready-for-dev
4	
5	## Story
6	
7	As a skill author,
8	I want a written evidence report when the soft gate falls back from its target threshold to the 80% floor,
9	So that the quality compromise is documented and never silent.
10	
11	## Acceptance Criteria
12	
13	1. **Given** a skill that scores 82% against a 90% target and remediation has been attempted
14	   **When** TS falls back from the 90% target to the 80% floor
15	   **Then** an evidence report is written to `{forge_version}/evidence-report-fallback.md`
16	   **And** the report contains: attempted threshold, achieved score, specific findings preventing the higher threshold, remediation steps attempted, and the final accepted threshold
17	
18	2. **Given** a fallback occurs
19	   **When** the pipeline completes
20	   **Then** the fallback is visible in the pipeline output (not buried in the report alone)
21	   **And** the pipeline output includes the path to the evidence report
22	
23	3. **Given** a skill that meets its target threshold without fallback
24	   **When** TS completes scoring
25	   **Then** no evidence report is generated
26	
27	## Tasks / Subtasks
28	
29	- [ ] Task 1: Add §4b threshold fallback logic to score.md (AC: #1, #3)
30	  - [ ] 1.1 Insert new §4b section between §4 (Determine Result) and §5 (Next Workflow Recommendation)
31	  - [ ] 1.2 Implement fallback detection: if `result == "FAIL"` AND `totalScore >= 80` AND `effective_threshold > 80`, trigger fallback
32	  - [ ] 1.3 On fallback trigger: override `result` to `"PASS"`, set `effective_threshold` to `80`, record `threshold_fallback: true`, `original_threshold`, `fallback_threshold: 80`, `evidence_report_path` in workflow context
33	  - [ ] 1.4 Do NOT trigger fallback if `result == "INCONCLUSIVE"` — the evidence-floor verdict is never overridden by fallback
34	  - [ ] 1.5 Do NOT trigger fallback if score < 80 — that's a genuine FAIL
35	  - [ ] 1.6 Do NOT trigger fallback if `effective_threshold == 80` — there's nothing to fall back from
36	
37	- [ ] Task 2: Generate evidence report file within §4b (AC: #1)
38	  - [ ] 2.1 Write `{forge_version}/evidence-report-fallback.md` with structured content (see Dev Notes for template)
39	  - [ ] 2.2 Read gap entries from `{outputFile}` Coverage Analysis and Coherence Analysis sections — list the specific findings that prevented the higher threshold (gaps with severity Critical through Low)
40	  - [ ] 2.3 Check for a prior test report in `{forge_version}/` by globbing `test-report-{skill_name}-*.md` — if found, note that a prior test run exists (implying remediation was attempted between runs); if not found, note "first test run — no prior remediation cycle"
41	  - [ ] 2.4 Include in the report: attempted threshold, achieved score, gap summary, remediation context, final accepted threshold (80%), threshold source, and the date
42	
43	- [ ] Task 3: Update score.md §6/§7/§8 to surface fallback (AC: #2)
44	  - [ ] 3.1 In §6 (Append Completeness Score to Output), add a `**Threshold Fallback:**` line after `**Threshold Source:**` when `threshold_fallback: true` — include original threshold, achieved score, and evidence report path
45	  - [ ] 3.2 In §7 (Update Output Frontmatter), add `thresholdFallback: true`, `originalThreshold: '{N}%'`, `evidenceReportPath: '{path}'` fields when fallback occurred
46	  - [ ] 3.3 In §8 (Report Score), append a prominent fallback notice in the pipeline output: "**Threshold fallback:** scored {score}% against {original}% target — accepted at 80% floor. Evidence report: {path}"
47	
48	- [ ] Task 4: Update report.md to surface fallback in final presentation (AC: #2)
49	  - [ ] 4.1 In §6 (Present Final Report), add a fallback notice block after the Result line when `thresholdFallback` is present in output frontmatter
50	  - [ ] 4.2 In §4c (Result Contract), add `threshold_fallback`, `original_threshold`, and `evidence_report_path` fields to the on-disk JSON payload when fallback occurred
51	
52	- [ ] Task 5: Update SKILL.md documentation (AC: #1, #2)
53	  - [ ] 5.1 Update the Outputs table to mention the evidence report artifact (`evidence-report-fallback.md` written under `{forge_version}/` when threshold fallback occurs)
54	  - [ ] 5.2 Update the Result Contract section to include `threshold_fallback`, `original_threshold`, `evidence_report_path` fields in the JSON schema description
55	  - [ ] 5.3 Update the headless envelope documentation to include fallback fields
56	
57	- [ ] Task 6: Create structural integration tests (AC: #1, #2, #3)
58	  - [ ] 6.1 Create `test/test-skf-evidence-report-fallback.py` following the pattern from `test/test-skf-per-pipeline-thresholds.py`
59	  - [ ] 6.2 Register the new test file in `package.json` test:python command
60	  - [ ] 6.3 Test cases: §4b section exists in score.md, fallback trigger conditions documented, evidence report path matches architecture spec, INCONCLUSIVE guard clause present, 80% floor constant present, fallback fields in output frontmatter (§7), fallback notice in score report (§8), SKILL.md outputs mention evidence report, result contract includes fallback fields, report.md §6 references fallback notice
61	
62	## Dev Notes
63	
64	### Threshold Fallback Logic — Where It Lives
65	
66	The fallback is implemented entirely in `score.md` as a new §4b between the existing §4 (Determine Result) and §5 (Next Workflow Recommendation). The logic:
67	
68	```
69	IF result == "FAIL"
70	  AND totalScore >= 80
71	  AND effective_threshold > 80
72	  AND result != "INCONCLUSIVE"  (redundant but explicit — INCONCLUSIVE is never FAIL)
73	THEN
74	  → THRESHOLD FALLBACK triggered
75	  → Generate evidence report
76	  → Override result to "PASS"
77	  → Set effective_threshold = 80 for §5/§6/§7/§8
78	  → Record threshold_fallback = true in workflow context
79	```
80	
81	The fallback does NOT fire when:
82	- Score >= effective_threshold (already PASS — no fallback needed)
83	- Score < 80 (genuine FAIL — floor not met)
84	- effective_threshold == 80 (nothing to fall back from)
85	- Result is INCONCLUSIVE (evidence-floor verdict never overridden)
86	- Post-score caps (§3d) forced FAIL with totalScore >= 80 AND effective_threshold > 80 — the fallback DOES apply here because the cap penalized the score for tooling degradation, not quality; the evidence report should note the cap was active
87	
88	### Interaction with Post-Score Caps (§3d)
89	
90	If Cap 1 or Cap 2 fired (tooling degraded, docs-only without validators), the effective_score was capped to `threshold - 1`. If the original effective_threshold was, say, 90, then effective_score was capped to 89. Since 89 >= 80, the fallback fires. The evidence report MUST note the cap in the remediation section: "Score capped due to {cap reason} — address tooling to test at the higher threshold."
91	
92	### Evidence Report Template
93	
94	The evidence report at `{forge_version}/evidence-report-fallback.md` should contain:
95	
96	```markdown
97	# Evidence Report: Threshold Fallback
98	
99	**Skill:** {skill_name}
100	**Date:** {ISO-8601 timestamp}
101	**Run ID:** {run_id}
102	
103	## Threshold Summary
104	
105	| Field | Value |
106	|-------|-------|
107	| Attempted Threshold | {original_threshold}% |
108	| Achieved Score | {totalScore}% |
109	| Threshold Source | {threshold_source} |
110	| Final Accepted Threshold | 80% |
111	
112	## Findings Preventing Higher Threshold
113	
114	{For each gap entry from Coverage Analysis and Coherence Analysis:}
115	- **{GAP-NNN}: {title}** — Severity: {severity}, Category: {category}
116	
117	{Count: N critical, M high, P medium, Q low, R info findings}
118	
119	## Remediation Context
120	
121	{If prior test report exists:}
122	A prior test run was found at `{prior_report_path}`, indicating remediation was attempted between runs.
123	
124	{If no prior test report:}
125	No prior test report found for this skill version — this is the first test run.
126	
127	{If post-score cap was active:}
128	**Note:** Score was capped due to {cap_reason}. Address tooling limitations to test at the higher threshold.
129	
130	## Conclusion
131	
132	Skill accepted at 80% floor (original target: {original_threshold}%). The {N} findings above prevented meeting the higher threshold. Review and address findings before the next pipeline run to achieve the {original_threshold}% target.
133	```
134	
135	### How `{forge_version}` Resolves
136	
137	`{forge_version}` resolves to the versioned skill data path — typically `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/{resolved_version}/`. This is the same directory where test reports (`test-report-{skill_name}-{run_id}.md`) are written. The evidence report sits alongside test reports for the same skill version, which is the correct semantic grouping.
138	
139	### Pipeline Output Visibility (AC #2)
140	
141	The fallback must be visible in THREE places:
142	1. **score.md §8 inline report** — the console output the user sees immediately after scoring
143	2. **report.md §6 final presentation** — the closing summary
144	3. **The evidence report file itself** — the persistent record
145	
146	The §8 report output adds a line like:
147	```
148	**Threshold fallback:** scored 82% against 90% target — accepted at 80% floor. Evidence report: {forge_version}/evidence-report-fallback.md
149	```
150	
151	### Result Contract Extension
152	
153	The on-disk JSON result contract (written in report.md §4c) gains three optional fields:
154	
155	```json
156	{
157	  "threshold_fallback": true,
158	  "original_threshold": 90,
159	  "evidence_report_path": "{forge_version}/evidence-report-fallback.md"
160	}
161	```
162	
163	These fields are absent when no fallback occurred (not `false`/`null` — absent). The headless stdout envelope adds `"threshold_fallback":true,"original_threshold":90` when fallback is active, omits them otherwise.
164	
165	### Step Chain — No New Step File
166	
167	This story does NOT create a new step file. The fallback logic lives inside score.md as a new §4b section. The step chain is unchanged:
168	
169	```
170	init.md → detect-mode.md → coverage-check.md → coherence-check.md → external-validators.md → step-hard-gate.md → score.md → report.md → health-check.md
171	```
172	
173	### What Must NOT Change
174	
175	- `step-hard-gate.md` — hard gate is severity-based, threshold-independent, unaffected by fallback
176	- `scoring-rules.md` — scoring rules don't change; gap severity definitions unaffected
177	- `coverage-check.md` / `coherence-check.md` — analysis steps unaffected; they still write findings in the same format
178	- `compute-score.py` — the scoring script still computes the same score; the fallback is a post-script evaluation decision
179	- `init.md` — threshold resolution in §1/§1b is unaffected; the fallback consumes the resolved threshold, doesn't change resolution
180	- `customize.toml` — `default_threshold = 80` remains the org-wide default; the 80% floor is a hardcoded constant in score.md, not configurable (the floor is architecturally fixed per FR-13)
181	- `external-validators.md` — validation step unaffected
182	- Exit codes — no new exit codes; a fallback PASS is still exit 0 (the skill passed, even if at a lower threshold)
183	
184	### Story 3.2 Learnings (Apply These)
185	
186	1. **Self-audit for internal references**: Do NOT include internal story IDs, epic numbers, or bmad keywords in the step file content or commit messages
187	2. **Cross-reference grep when modifying step files**: After modifying score.md and report.md, grep for any other files that reference `thresholdSource`, `testResult`, or the result contract schema to ensure consistency
188	3. **Section numbering convention**: Use §-prefixed sections within step files (§1, §2, etc.) — the new section in score.md should be §4b to slot between existing §4 and §5
189	4. **Backward compatibility**: Verify the fallback does NOT change behavior for standalone TS invocations at 80% threshold (no pipeline default → effective_threshold == 80 → no fallback possible → same behavior as before)
190	5. **Threshold source logging**: The fallback modifies the threshold but the `threshold_source` from §1 remains unchanged — it records where the *original* threshold came from; the evidence report records the fallback decision separately
191	
192	### Cross-Reference Check
193	
194	After modifying score.md and report.md, verify these for consistency:
195	- `src/skf-test-skill/SKILL.md` — Outputs table should mention evidence report; Result Contract should include fallback fields
196	- `src/skf-test-skill/references/report.md` — §4c result contract JSON should include fallback fields; §6 presentation should surface fallback
197	- `src/shared/references/pipeline-contracts.md` — Circuit Breakers table for TS should note that scores between 80% and the pipeline default produce a fallback PASS (not a circuit breaker halt)
198	- `src/skf-test-skill/references/score.md` — §6/§7 should reference fallback state from §4b
199	
200	### Pipeline Circuit Breaker Interaction
201	
202	When TS runs in a pipeline with a per-pipeline threshold (e.g., deepwiki → 90%), and the score falls to 82%, TS internally falls back to 80% and reports PASS. The forger's circuit breaker checks the TS result: since TS reports PASS (with fallback notation), the pipeline **continues to EX** — it does not halt. The evidence report is the audit trail for why the quality compromise was accepted.
203	
204	If the score falls below 80%, TS reports FAIL with no fallback, and the circuit breaker halts the pipeline as normal.
205	
206	### Project Structure Notes

209	- `src/skf-test-skill/references/score.md` — gains §4b (threshold fallback + evidence report generation)

file.content:
# Story 3.3: Evidence Report on Threshold Fallback

Status: ready-for-dev

## Story

As a skill author,
I want a written evidence report when the soft gate falls back from its target threshold to the 80% floor,
So that the quality compromise is documented and never silent.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** a skill that scores 82% against a 90% target and remediation has been attempted
   **When** TS falls back from the 90% target to the 80% floor
   **Then** an evidence report is written to `{forge_version}/evidence-report-fallback.md`
   **And** the report contains: attempted threshold, achieved score, specific findings preventing the higher threshold, remediation steps attempted, and the final accepted threshold

2. **Given** a fallback occurs
   **When** the pipeline completes
   **Then** the fallback is visible in the pipeline output (not buried in the report alone)
   **And** the pipeline output includes the path to the evidence report

3. **Given** a skill that meets its target threshold without fallback
   **When** TS completes scoring
   **Then** no evidence report is generated

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [ ] Task 1: Add §4b threshold fallback logic to score.md (AC: #1, #3)
  - [ ] 1.1 Insert new §4b section between §4 (Determine Result) and §5 (Next Workflow Recommendation)
  - [ ] 1.2 Implement fallback detection: if `result == "FAIL"` AND `totalScore >= 80` AND `effective_threshold > 80`, trigger fallback
  - [ ] 1.3 On fallback trigger: override `result` to `"PASS"`, set `effective_threshold` to `80`, record `threshold_fallback: true`, `original_threshold`, `fallback_threshold: 80`, `evidence_report_path` in workflow context
  - [ ] 1.4 Do NOT trigger fallback if `result == "INCONCLUSIVE"` — the evidence-floor verdict is never overridden by fallback
  - [ ] 1.5 Do NOT trigger fallback if score < 80 — that's a genuine FAIL
  - [ ] 1.6 Do NOT trigger fallback if `effective_threshold == 80` — there's nothing to fall back from

- [ ] Task 2: Generate evidence report file within §4b (AC: #1)
  - [ ] 2.1 Write `{forge_version}/evidence-report-fallback.md` with structured content (see Dev Notes for template)
  - [ ] 2.2 Read gap entries from `{outputFile}` Coverage Analysis and Coherence Analysis sections — list the specific findings that prevented the higher threshold (gaps with severity Critical through Low)
  - [ ] 2.3 Check for a prior test report in `{forge_version}/` by globbing `test-report-{skill_name}-*.md` — if found, note that a prior test run exists (implying remediation was attempted between runs); if not found, note "first test run — no prior remediation cycle"
  - [ ] 2.4 Include in the report: attempted threshold, achieved score, gap summary, remediation context, final accepted threshold (80%), threshold source, and the date

- [ ] Task 3: Update score.md §6/§7/§8 to surface fallback (AC: #2)
  - [ ] 3.1 In §6 (Append Completeness Score to Output), add a `**Threshold Fallback:**` line after `**Threshold Source:**` when `threshold_fallback: true` — include original threshold, achieved score, and evidence report path
  - [ ] 3.2 In §7 (Update Output Frontmatter), add `thresholdFallback: true`, `originalThreshold: '{N}%'`, `evidenceReportPath: '{path}'` fields when fallback occurred
  - [ ] 3.3 In §8 (Report Score), append a prominent fallback notice in the pipeline output: "**Threshold fallback:** scored {score}% against {original}% target — accepted at 80% floor. Evidence report: {path}"

- [ ] Task 4: Update report.md to surface fallback in final presentation (AC: #2)
  - [ ] 4.1 In §6 (Present Final Report), add a fallback notice block after the Result line when `thresholdFallback` is present in output frontmatter
  - [ ] 4.2 In §4c (Result Contract), add `threshold_fallback`, `original_threshold`, and `evidence_report_path` fields to the on-disk JSON payload when fallback occurred

- [ ] Task 5: Update SKILL.md documentation (AC: #1, #2)
  - [ ] 5.1 Update the Outputs table to mention the evidence report artifact (`evidence-report-fallback.md` written under `{forge_version}/` when threshold fallback occurs)
  - [ ] 5.2 Update the Result Contract section to include `threshold_fallback`, `original_threshold`, `evidence_report_path` fields in the JSON schema description
  - [ ] 5.3 Update the headless envelope documentation to include fallback fields

- [ ] Task 6: Create structural integration tests (AC: #1, #2, #3)
  - [ ] 6.1 Create `test/test-skf-evidence-report-fallback.py` following the pattern from `test/test-skf-per-pipeline-thresholds.py`
  - [ ] 6.2 Register the new test file in `package.json` test:python command
  - [ ] 6.3 Test cases: §4b section exists in score.md, fallback trigger conditions documented, evidence report path matches architecture spec, INCONCLUSIVE guard clause present, 80% floor constant present, fallback fields in output frontmatter (§7), fallback notice in score report (§8), SKILL.md outputs mention evidence report, result contract includes fallback fields, report.md §6 references fallback notice

## Dev Notes

### Threshold Fallback Logic — Where It Lives

The fallback is implemented entirely in `score.md` as a new §4b between the existing §4 (Determine Result) and §5 (Next Workflow Recommendation). The logic:

```
IF result == "FAIL"
  AND totalScore >= 80
  AND effective_threshold > 80
  AND result != "INCONCLUSIVE"  (redundant but explicit — INCONCLUSIVE is never FAIL)
THEN
  → THRESHOLD FALLBACK triggered
  → Generate evidence report
  → Override result to "PASS"
  → Set effective_threshold = 80 for §5/§6/§7/§8
  → Record threshold_fallback = true in workflow context
```

The fallback does NOT fire when:
- Score >= effective_threshold (already PASS — no fallback needed)
- Score < 80 (genuine FAIL — floor not met)
- effective_threshold == 80 (nothing to fall back from)
- Result is INCONCLUSIVE (evidence-floor verdict never overridden)
- Post-score caps (§3d) forced FAIL with totalScore >= 80 AND effective_threshold > 80 — the fallback DOES apply here because the cap penalized the score for tooling degradation, not quality; the evidence report should note the cap was active

### Interaction with Post-Score Caps (§3d)

If Cap 1 or Cap 2 fired (tooling degraded, docs-only without validators), the effective_score was capped to `threshold - 1`. If the original effective_threshold was, say, 90, then effective_score was capped to 89. Since 89 >= 80, the fallback fires. The evidence report MUST note the cap in the remediation section: "Score capped due to {cap reason} — address tooling to test at the higher threshold."

### Evidence Report Template

The evidence report at `{forge_version}/evidence-report-fallback.md` should contain:

```markdown
# Evidence Report: Threshold Fallback

**Skill:** {skill_name}
**Date:** {ISO-8601 timestamp}
**Run ID:** {run_id}

## Threshold Summary

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Attempted Threshold | {original_threshold}% |
| Achieved Score | {totalScore}% |
| Threshold Source | {threshold_source} |
| Final Accepted Threshold | 80% |

## Findings Preventing Higher Threshold

{For each gap entry from Coverage Analysis and Coherence Analysis:}
- **{GAP-NNN}: {title}** — Severity: {severity}, Category: {category}

{Count: N critical, M high, P medium, Q low, R info findings}

## Remediation Context

{If prior test report exists:}
A prior test run was found at `{prior_report_path}`, indicating remediation was attempted between runs.

{If no prior test report:}
No prior test report found for this skill version — this is the first test run.

{If post-score cap was active:}
**Note:** Score was capped due to {cap_reason}. Address tooling limitations to test at the higher threshold.

## Conclusion

Skill accepted at 80% floor (original target: {original_threshold}%). The {N} findings above prevented meeting the higher threshold. Review and address findings before the next pipeline run to achieve the {original_threshold}% target.
```

### How `{forge_version}` Resolves

`{forge_version}` resolves to the versioned skill data path — typically `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/{resolved_version}/`. This is the same directory where test reports (`test-report-{skill_name}-{run_id}.md`) are written. The evidence report sits alongside test reports for the same skill version, which is the correct semantic grouping.

### Pipeline Output Visibility (AC #2)

The fallback must be visible in THREE places:
1. **score.md §8 inline report** — the console output the user sees immediately after scoring
2. **report.md §6 final presentation** — the closing summary
3. **The evidence report file itself** — the persistent record

The §8 report output adds a line like:
```
**Threshold fallback:** scored 82% against 90% target — accepted at 80% floor. Evidence report: {forge_version}/evidence-report-fallback.md
```

### Result Contract Extension

The on-disk JSON result contract (written in report.md §4c) gains three optional fields:

```json
{
  "threshold_fallback": true,
  "original_threshold": 90,
  "evidence_report_path": "{forge_version}/evidence-report-fallback.md"
}
```

These fields are absent when no fallback occurred (not `false`/`null` — absent). The headless stdout envelope adds `"threshold_fallback":true,"original_threshold":90` when fallback is active, omits them otherwise.

### Step Chain — No New Step File

This story does NOT create a new step file. The fallback logic lives inside score.md as a new §4b section. The step chain is unchanged:

```
init.md → detect-mode.md → coverage-check.md → coherence-check.md → external-validators.md → step-hard-gate.md → score.md → report.md → health-check.md
```

### What Must NOT Change

- `step-hard-gate.md` — hard gate is severity-based, threshold-independent, unaffected by fallback
- `scoring-rules.md` — scoring rules don't change; gap severity definitions unaffected
- `coverage-check.md` / `coherence-check.md` — analysis steps unaffected; they still write findings in the same format
- `compute-score.py` — the scoring script still computes the same score; the fallback is a post-script evaluation decision
- `init.md` — threshold resolution in §1/§1b is unaffected; the fallback consumes the resolved threshold, doesn't change resolution
- `customize.toml` — `default_threshold = 80` remains the org-wide default; the 80% floor is a hardcoded constant in score.md, not configurable (the floor is architecturally fixed per FR-13)
- `external-validators.md` — validation step unaffected
- Exit codes — no new exit codes; a fallback PASS is still exit 0 (the skill passed, even if at a lower threshold)

### Story 3.2 Learnings (Apply These)

1. **Self-audit for internal references**: Do NOT include internal story IDs, epic numbers, or bmad keywords in the step file content or commit messages
2. **Cross-reference grep when modifying step files**: After modifying score.md and report.md, grep for any other files that reference `thresholdSource`, `testResult`, or the result contract schema to ensure consistency
3. **Section numbering convention**: Use §-prefixed sections within step files (§1, §2, etc.) — the new section in score.md should be §4b to slot between existing §4 and §5
4. **Backward compatibility**: Verify the fallback does NOT change behavior for standalone TS invocations at 80% threshold (no pipeline default → effective_threshold == 80 → no fallback possible → same behavior as before)
5. **Threshold source logging**: The fallback modifies the threshold but the `threshold_source` from §1 remains unchanged — it records where the *original* threshold came from; the evidence report records the fallback decision separately

### Cross-Reference Check

After modifying score.md and report.md, verify these for consistency:
- `src/skf-test-skill/SKILL.md` — Outputs table should mention evidence report; Result Contract should include fallback fields
- `src/skf-test-skill/references/report.md` — §4c result contract JSON should include fallback fields; §6 presentation should surface fallback
- `src/shared/references/pipeline-contracts.md` — Circuit Breakers table for TS should note that scores between 80% and the pipeline default produce a fallback PASS (not a circuit breaker halt)
- `src/skf-test-skill/references/score.md` — §6/§7 should reference fallback state from §4b

### Pipeline Circuit Breaker Interaction

When TS runs in a pipeline with a per-pipeline threshold (e.g., deepwiki → 90%), and the score falls to 82%, TS internally falls back to 80% and reports PASS. The forger's circuit breaker checks the TS result: since TS reports PASS (with fallback notation), the pipeline **continues to EX** — it does not halt. The evidence report is the audit trail for why the quality compromise was accepted.

If the score falls below 80%, TS reports FAIL with no fallback, and the circuit breaker halts the pipeline as normal.

### Project Structure Notes

- No new files created in `src/` — only modifications to existing step files
- `src/skf-test-skill/references/score.md` — gains §4b (threshold fallback + evidence report generation)
- `src/skf-test-skill/references/report.md` — §6 gains fallback notice, §4c gains fallback fields in result contract
- `src/skf-test-skill/SKILL.md` — Outputs and Result Contract documentation updated
- `test/test-skf-evidence-report-fallback.py` — new structural integration test file
- `package.json` — test:python command updated to include new test file
- Runtime artifact: `{forge_version}/evidence-report-fallback.md` — written at test time only when fallback occurs

### References

- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md — Story 3.3 "Evidence report on threshold fallback"]
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md — "Quality Gate Architecture" section, line 222-226: evidence report spec]

- [Source: src/skf-test-skill/references/report.md — §4c result contract, §6 final presentation]
- [Source: src/skf-test-skill/references/step-hard-gate.md — threshold-independent hard gate (unchanged)]
- [Source: src/skf-test-skill/references/init.md — §1b per-pipeline threshold lookup table (unchanged)]

- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-2-per-pipeline-quality-thresholds.md — Story 3.2 learnings]

13	## STEP GOAL:

25	1. If the workflow received `--threshold=<N>` on invocation, use that integer as `effective_threshold` (CLI wins). Set `threshold_source` = `"CLI override ({N}%)"`.
26	2. Else if `{pipeline_default_threshold}` is set in workflow context (resolved by init.md §1b from the per-pipeline threshold lookup table when `{pipeline_alias}` is present), use it as `effective_threshold`. Set `threshold_source` = `"pipeline default ({pipeline_alias} → {N}%)"`.
27	3. Else if the resolved `{defaultThreshold}` workflow-context variable (from SKILL.md On Activation §3 — `workflow.default_threshold` scalar, default `80`) is set, use it as `effective_threshold`. Set `threshold_source` = `"workflow default ({N}%)"`.
28	4. Else fall back to `80` (the bundled default — this branch should be unreachable when SKILL.md resolution ran correctly, but keeps the step robust if customize.toml resolution failed silently). Set `threshold_source` = `"bundled fallback (80%)"`.
29	
30	Store `threshold_source` in workflow context for use in the score report section.
31	
32	Pass `effective_threshold` into the scoring-input JSON's `threshold` field in §3a (the compute-score.py script already honors this field). The CLI flag, pipeline default, and the scalar all feed the same downstream field; the script does not need to know which layer supplied the value.
33	
34	**Docs-only mode check:** If the Coverage Analysis section in `{outputFile}` notes docs-only mode (set by step 3 for skills with all `[EXT:...]` citations and no local source), apply Quick-tier weight redistribution: Signature Accuracy and Type Coverage are not scored, their weights (22% + 14%) are redistributed proportionally to remaining active categories. Coverage score is based on documentation completeness rather than source coverage (as calculated by step 3).
35	
36	### 2. Read Category Scores from Output
37	
38	Read `{outputFile}` and extract the category scores calculated in previous steps:
39	
40	**From Coverage Analysis (step 03):**
41	- Export Coverage: {percentage}%
42	- Signature Accuracy: {percentage}% or N/A (Quick tier)
43	- Type Coverage: {percentage}% or N/A (Quick tier)
44	
45	**From Coherence Analysis (step 04):**
46	- Combined Coherence: {percentage}% (contextual mode only)
47	- Or: not scored (naive mode — weight redistributed)
48	
49	**From External Validation (step 04b):**
50	- External Validation Score: {percentage}% (combined skill-check + tessl average)
51	- Or: N/A (if neither tool was available — weight redistributed to other categories)
52	
53	### 3. Apply Weight Distribution
54	
55	**Read testMode from {outputFile} frontmatter.**
56	
57	#### 2b. Apply State 2 Undercount Deduction (pre-script)
58	
59	If `analysis_confidence == 'provenance-map'` (State 2) AND `metadata.json.skill_type != "stack"` AND step 3 recorded a provenance vs metadata divergence > 5% (see §4b in step 3), apply a 10-point deduction to `exportCoverage` BEFORE building the scoring input:
60	
61	```
62	exportCoverage_adjusted = max(0, exportCoverage - 10)
63	```
64	
65	Record in the report: `scoring_notes: State 2 undercount risk acknowledged — 10% deduction applied to Export Coverage (raw: {N}%, adjusted: {M}%)`. Use the adjusted value as the `exportCoverage` field in §3a below. The deduction is deterministic and does not change category weights or active-category counting.
66	
67	Stack skills are exempt (the `metadata.json.skill_type != "stack"` guard above; `skill_type` is loaded in step 01 and also surfaces as `stackSkill` in §3a): a stack's own barrel is empty by design, so a provenance-vs-metadata divergence on a stack reflects the barrel-vs-constituent surface difference (suppressed by §4b's stack-skill branch in step 3), not extraction undercount — deducting for it would penalize a correctly built stack.
68	
69	#### 3a. Construct Scoring Input JSON
70	
71	Build a JSON object from the data gathered in steps 1-2:
72	
73	```json
74	{
75	  "mode": "{testMode: contextual or naive}",
76	  "tier": "{forge_tier: Quick, Forge, Forge+, or Deep}",
77	  "docsOnly": "{true if docs_only_mode detected in step 03, else false}",
78	  "state2": "{true if analysis_confidence is provenance-map, else false}",
79	  "stackSkill": "{true if metadata.json.skill_type == 'stack', else false}",
80	  "referenceApp": "{true if metadata.json.scope_type == 'reference-app', else false}",
81	  "scores": {
82	    "exportCoverage": "{export_coverage_percentage}",
83	    "signatureAccuracy": "{signature_accuracy_percentage or null if N/A}",
84	    "typeCoverage": "{type_coverage_percentage or null if N/A}",
85	    "coherence": "{combined_coherence_percentage or null if naive mode}",
86	    "externalValidation": "{external_validation_score or null if N/A}"
87	  },
88	  "threshold": "{effective_threshold from §1 — CLI --threshold wins, then pipeline default, then workflow.default_threshold scalar, then 80}"
89	}
90	```
91	
92	**Important:** Score values must be numbers (not strings). Use `null` (not `"N/A"`) for categories that were not scored. Read `metadata.json.skill_type` from `{resolved_skill_package}/metadata.json`; if the value is `"stack"`, set `stackSkill: true` and pass `null` for `signatureAccuracy` and `typeCoverage` (the categories will be redistributed per `{scoringRulesFile}` Stack Skills rule). Likewise read `metadata.json.scope_type`; if the value is `"reference-app"`, set `referenceApp: true` and pass `null` for `signatureAccuracy` and `typeCoverage` (redistributed per `{scoringRulesFile}` Reference-App rule — a reference app documents wiring patterns, not library export signatures).
93	
94	#### 3b. Run the Scoring Script
95	
96	```bash
97	echo '<JSON>' | uv run {scoringScript} --stdin
98	```
99	
100	Where `{scoringScript}` is the path resolved from the frontmatter variable (relative to the skill root, i.e., the skf-test-skill/ directory). The script also accepts the JSON as a positional argument (`uv run {scoringScript} '<JSON>'`) or via `--json-input '<JSON>'`; `--stdin` is preferred since it avoids shell-quote escaping of nested JSON.
101	
102	Parse the JSON output. The script returns:
103	- `weights` — final redistributed weights per category
104	- `weightedScores` — weighted contribution per category
105	- `totalScore` — the overall completeness score
106	- `threshold` — the threshold used
107	- `result` — `"PASS"`, `"FAIL"`, or **`"INCONCLUSIVE"`** (minimum-evidence floor — see `{scoringRulesFile}`)
108	- `activeCategories` — list of categories that were scored
109	- `skippedCategories` — list of categories that were skipped
110	- `skipReasons` — why each category was skipped
111	- `weightSum` — sum of final weights (should be ~100)
112	- `inconclusiveReasons` — only present when `result == "INCONCLUSIVE"`; explains which floor clause tripped
113	
114	Use these values for Section 4 (pass/fail/inconclusive) and Section 6 (output formatting). **Never override the script's `result` value** — floor enforcement is centralized in the script for determinism.
115	
116	#### 3c. Fallback (if script execution fails)
117	
118	If the script is unavailable or returns an error, fall back to manual calculation:
119	
120	1. Select the weight table from `{scoringRulesFile}` for the detected mode (naive or contextual)
121	2. Determine skip conditions: Quick tier/docsOnly/state2/stackSkill/referenceApp skip Signature Accuracy + Type Coverage; naive mode coherence is already 0; null external validation means skip it
122	3. For each skipped category, set its weight to 0
123	4. Compute sum of active category weights
124	5. For each active category: `new_weight = old_weight / sum_active * 100`
125	6. `weighted_score = new_weight / 100 * category_score`
126	7. `total = sum of all weighted_scores`
127	
128	Report: "**Note:** Scoring script unavailable — calculated manually per scoring-rules.md."
129	
130	### 3d. Apply Post-Score Caps (BEFORE PASS/FAIL determination)
131	
132	The scoring script returns `totalScore` and a preliminary `result`. Two caps may override the script's PASS into FAIL — applied in this order, and ONLY if the script did NOT return INCONCLUSIVE (floor always takes precedence):
133	
134	**Cap 1 — Tooling degraded:** if `analysisConfidence == "degraded"` or `toolingStatus` indicates a missing helper (`python3-missing`, `frontmatter-validator-missing`), set `effective_score = min(totalScore, threshold - 1)` and record `scoring_notes: tooling degraded — capped below threshold until helper restored`.
135	
136	**Cap 2 — Docs-only without external validators:** if `docsOnly == true` AND `externalValidation` score was null (neither skill-check nor tessl ran), set `effective_score = min(effective_score, threshold - 1)` and record `scoring_notes: docs-only without external validators — capped below threshold`.
137	
138	If either cap fired and `result` was `PASS`, change it to `FAIL`. If `result` was already `INCONCLUSIVE`, leave it — the evidence-floor verdict is never overridden by a cap.
139	
140	### 4. Determine Result (PASS / FAIL / INCONCLUSIVE)
141	
142	The scoring script enforces the minimum-evidence floor BEFORE comparing score vs threshold. Three possible outcomes:
143	
144	```
145	IF result == "INCONCLUSIVE" — minimum-evidence floor tripped; not PASS, not FAIL
146	IF score >= threshold       → PASS
147	IF score < threshold        → FAIL
148	```
149	
150	**INCONCLUSIVE floor clauses** (see `{scoringRulesFile}`):
151	- `active_categories < 2` (after all redistribution), OR
152	- `tier == "Quick"` AND Export Coverage is the sole scoring contributor
153	
154	**Tooling-degraded cap:** If the `analysisConfidence` in output frontmatter is `degraded` (python3 missing, frontmatter validator missing, or other degraded state flagged in step 1), the step MUST cap the score at `threshold - 1` BEFORE the PASS/FAIL comparison. This forces a deterministic FAIL until tooling is restored. Do NOT override an INCONCLUSIVE result with the cap — INCONCLUSIVE remains the verdict.
155	
156	### 5. Determine Next Workflow Recommendation
157	
158	Based on test result:
159	
160	**IF PASS:**
161	- `nextWorkflow: 'export-skill'` — skill is ready for export
162	- **Drift override:** if workflow context carries
163	  `allow_workspace_drift: true` (set in step 1 §5b when the user passed
164	  `--allow-workspace-drift` AND the workspace HEAD did not match
165	  `metadata.source_commit`), the PASS is a **conditional PASS**:
166	  - Write `testResult: 'pass-with-drift'` to the output frontmatter instead of
167	    bare `'pass'`. The result contract (§4c of step 6) mirrors the same
168	    value.
169	  - Override `nextWorkflow` to `'update-skill'` — **refuse to recommend
170	    `export-skill`**. The drift override weakens the workflow's strongest
171	    false-positive guard (we tested against HEAD, not the pinned source); a
172	    PASS under drift is not trustworthy enough to promote to export without a
173	    clean re-test against the pinned commit.
174	  - Record `scoring_notes: workspace drift overridden — PASS is conditional; re-run against pinned commit before export`.
175	
176	**IF FAIL:**
177	- `nextWorkflow: 'update-skill'` — skill needs remediation before export
178	
179	**IF INCONCLUSIVE:**
180	- `nextWorkflow: 'manual-review'` — evidence base is insufficient to grade the skill automatically. The test report records `inconclusiveReasons` from the scoring script. Surface to the user — do not auto-recommend export or update.
181	
182	### 6. Append Completeness Score to Output
183	
184	Append the **Completeness Score** section to `{outputFile}`:
185	
186	```markdown
187	## Completeness Score
188	
189	### Score Breakdown
190	
191	| Category | Score | Weight | Weighted |
192	|----------|-------|--------|----------|
193	| Export Coverage | {N}% | {W}% | {WS}% |
194	| Signature Accuracy | {N}% | {W}% | {WS}% |
195	| Type Coverage | {N}% | {W}% | {WS}% |
196	| Coherence | {N}% | {W}% | {WS}% |
197	| External Validation | {N}% | {W}% | {WS}% |
198	| **Total** | | **100%** | **{total}%** |
199	
200	### Result
201	
202	**Score:** {total}%
203	**Threshold:** {threshold}%
204	**Result:** **{PASS|FAIL|INCONCLUSIVE}**
205	{If INCONCLUSIVE:}
206	**Inconclusive Reasons:**
207	{bulleted list from script `inconclusiveReasons`}
208	
209	**Threshold Source:** {threshold_source}
210	**Weight Distribution:** {naive (redistributed) | contextual (full)}
211	**Tier Adjustment:** {none | Quick tier — signature and type coverage not scored}
212	**External Validators:** {both available | skill-check only | tessl only | none — weight redistributed}
213	**Analysis Confidence:** {full | provenance-map | metadata-only | remote-only | docs-only}
214	```
215	
216	If `analysis_confidence` is not `full`, append a degradation notice. **The notice must be confidence-aware** — see the degradation notice rules in `{sourceAccessProtocol}`:
217	
218	```markdown
219	### Access Degradation Notice
220	
221	**Resolved via:** {analysis_confidence} {confidence breakdown if provenance-map, e.g., "(T1 AST-verified at compilation time)" or "(12 T1, 3 T1-low)"}
222	**Impact:** {describe limitation — e.g., "Signature checks limited to name-matching. Source file:line citations from provenance-map, not live AST." — or "Provenance data is at highest confidence; no limitation." for all-T1 provenance-map}
223	**Recommendation:** {confidence-dependent — see {sourceAccessProtocol} degradation notice rules. Do NOT recommend local clone when provenance-map entries are already T1.}
224	```
225	
226	### 7. Update Output Frontmatter
227	
228	Update `{outputFile}` frontmatter:
229	- `testResult: '{pass|pass-with-drift|fail|inconclusive}'` (lowercase; mirrors script `result`, with `pass-with-drift` substituted for `pass` when `allow_workspace_drift` was set and drift was observed — see §5 drift override)
230	- `score: '{total}%'`
231	- `threshold: '{threshold}%'`
232	- `thresholdSource: '{threshold_source}'`
233	- `analysisConfidence: '{full|degraded|provenance-map|metadata-only|remote-only|docs-only}'`
234	- `nextWorkflow: '{export-skill|update-skill|manual-review}'`
235	- Append `'score'` to `stepsCompleted`
236	
237	### 8. Report Score
238	
239	"**Completeness score calculated.**
240	
241	**{skill_name}:** **{total}%** — **{PASS|FAIL}**
242	
243	| Category | Score | Weighted |
244	|----------|-------|----------|
245	| Export Coverage | {N}% | {WS}% |
246	| Signature Accuracy | {N}% | {WS}% |
247	| Type Coverage | {N}% | {WS}% |
248	| Coherence | {N}% | {WS}% |
249	| External Validation | {N}% | {WS}% |
250	
251	**Threshold:** {threshold}%
252	**Recommendation:** {export-skill if pass | update-skill if fail}
253	
254	**Proceeding to gap report...**"
255	
256	Update stepsCompleted, then load and execute {nextStepFile}.
257	
258

file.content:
---
nextStepFile: 'report.md'
outputFile: '{forge_version}/test-report-{skill_name}-{run_id}.md'
scoringRulesFile: 'references/scoring-rules.md'
sourceAccessProtocol: 'references/source-access-protocol.md'
scoringScript: 'scripts/compute-score.py'
---

<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->

# Step 5: Score

## STEP GOAL:

Calculate the overall completeness score by aggregating coverage, coherence, and external validation category scores with the appropriate weight distribution (naive or contextual), apply the pass/fail threshold, and determine the test result.

### 1. Load Scoring Rules

Load `{scoringRulesFile}` to get:
- Category weights (naive vs contextual distribution)
- Tier-dependent scoring adjustments

**Resolve the pass threshold (precedence: CLI > pipeline default > scalar > bundled fallback):**

1. If the workflow received `--threshold=<N>` on invocation, use that integer as `effective_threshold` (CLI wins). Set `threshold_source` = `"CLI override ({N}%)"`.
2. Else if `{pipeline_default_threshold}` is set in workflow context (resolved by init.md §1b from the per-pipeline threshold lookup table when `{pipeline_alias}` is present), use it as `effective_threshold`. Set `threshold_source` = `"pipeline default ({pipeline_alias} → {N}%)"`.
3. Else if the resolved `{defaultThreshold}` workflow-context variable (from SKILL.md On Activation §3 — `workflow.default_threshold` scalar, default `80`) is set, use it as `effective_threshold`. Set `threshold_source` = `"workflow default ({N}%)"`.
4. Else fall back to `80` (the bundled default — this branch should be unreachable when SKILL.md resolution ran correctly, but keeps the step robust if customize.toml resolution failed silently). Set `threshold_source` = `"bundled fallback (80%)"`.

Store `threshold_source` in workflow context for use in the score report section.

Pass `effective_threshold` into the scoring-input JSON's `threshold` field in §3a (the compute-score.py script already honors this field). The CLI flag, pipeline default, and the scalar all feed the same downstream field; the script does not need to know which layer supplied the value.

**Docs-only mode check:** If the Coverage Analysis section in `{outputFile}` notes docs-only mode (set by step 3 for skills with all `[EXT:...]` citations and no local source), apply Quick-tier weight redistribution: Signature Accuracy and Type Coverage are not scored, their weights (22% + 14%) are redistributed proportionally to remaining active categories. Coverage score is based on documentation completeness rather than source coverage (as calculated by step 3).

### 2. Read Category Scores from Output

Read `{outputFile}` and extract the category scores calculated in previous steps:

**From Coverage Analysis (step 03):**
- Export Coverage: {percentage}%
- Signature Accuracy: {percentage}% or N/A (Quick tier)
- Type Coverage: {percentage}% or N/A (Quick tier)

**From Coherence Analysis (step 04):**
- Combined Coherence: {percentage}% (contextual mode only)
- Or: not scored (naive mode — weight redistributed)

**From External Validation (step 04b):**
- External Validation Score: {percentage}% (combined skill-check + tessl average)
- Or: N/A (if neither tool was available — weight redistributed to other categories)

### 3. Apply Weight Distribution

**Read testMode from {outputFile} frontmatter.**

#### 2b. Apply State 2 Undercount Deduction (pre-script)

If `analysis_confidence == 'provenance-map'` (State 2) AND `metadata.json.skill_type != "stack"` AND step 3 recorded a provenance vs metadata divergence > 5% (see §4b in step 3), apply a 10-point deduction to `exportCoverage` BEFORE building the scoring input:

```
exportCoverage_adjusted = max(0, exportCoverage - 10)
```

Record in the report: `scoring_notes: State 2 undercount risk acknowledged — 10% deduction applied to Export Coverage (raw: {N}%, adjusted: {M}%)`. Use the adjusted value as the `exportCoverage` field in §3a below. The deduction is deterministic and does not change category weights or active-category counting.

Stack skills are exempt (the `metadata.json.skill_type != "stack"` guard above; `skill_type` is loaded in step 01 and also surfaces as `stackSkill` in §3a): a stack's own barrel is empty by design, so a provenance-vs-metadata divergence on a stack reflects the barrel-vs-constituent surface difference (suppressed by §4b's stack-skill branch in step 3), not extraction undercount — deducting for it would penalize a correctly built stack.

#### 3a. Construct Scoring Input JSON

Build a JSON object from the data gathered in steps 1-2:

```json
{
  "mode": "{testMode: contextual or naive}",
  "tier": "{forge_tier: Quick, Forge, Forge+, or Deep}",
  "docsOnly": "{true if docs_only_mode detected in step 03, else false}",
  "state2": "{true if analysis_confidence is provenance-map, else false}",
  "stackSkill": "{true if metadata.json.skill_type == 'stack', else false}",
  "referenceApp": "{true if metadata.json.scope_type == 'reference-app', else false}",
  "scores": {
    "exportCoverage": "{export_coverage_percentage}",
    "signatureAccuracy": "{signature_accuracy_percentage or null if N/A}",
    "typeCoverage": "{type_coverage_percentage or null if N/A}",
    "coherence": "{combined_coherence_percentage or null if naive mode}",
    "externalValidation": "{external_validation_score or null if N/A}"
  },
  "threshold": "{effective_threshold from §1 — CLI --threshold wins, then pipeline default, then workflow.default_threshold scalar, then 80}"
}
```

**Important:** Score values must be numbers (not strings). Use `null` (not `"N/A"`) for categories that were not scored. Read `metadata.json.skill_type` from `{resolved_skill_package}/metadata.json`; if the value is `"stack"`, set `stackSkill: true` and pass `null` for `signatureAccuracy` and `typeCoverage` (the categories will be redistributed per `{scoringRulesFile}` Stack Skills rule). Likewise read `metadata.json.scope_type`; if the value is `"reference-app"`, set `referenceApp: true` and pass `null` for `signatureAccuracy` and `typeCoverage` (redistributed per `{scoringRulesFile}` Reference-App rule — a reference app documents wiring patterns, not library export signatures).

#### 3b. Run the Scoring Script

```bash
echo '<JSON>' | uv run {scoringScript} --stdin
```

Where `{scoringScript}` is the path resolved from the frontmatter variable (relative to the skill root, i.e., the skf-test-skill/ directory). The script also accepts the JSON as a positional argument (`uv run {scoringScript} '<JSON>'`) or via `--json-input '<JSON>'`; `--stdin` is preferred since it avoids shell-quote escaping of nested JSON.

Parse the JSON output. The script returns:
- `weights` — final redistributed weights per category
- `weightedScores` — weighted contribution per category
- `totalScore` — the overall completeness score
- `threshold` — the threshold used
- `result` — `"PASS"`, `"FAIL"`, or **`"INCONCLUSIVE"`** (minimum-evidence floor — see `{scoringRulesFile}`)
- `activeCategories` — list of categories that were scored
- `skippedCategories` — list of categories that were skipped
- `skipReasons` — why each category was skipped
- `weightSum` — sum of final weights (should be ~100)
- `inconclusiveReasons` — only present when `result == "INCONCLUSIVE"`; explains which floor clause tripped

Use these values for Section 4 (pass/fail/inconclusive) and Section 6 (output formatting). **Never override the script's `result` value** — floor enforcement is centralized in the script for determinism.

#### 3c. Fallback (if script execution fails)

If the script is unavailable or returns an error, fall back to manual calculation:

1. Select the weight table from `{scoringRulesFile}` for the detected mode (naive or contextual)
2. Determine skip conditions: Quick tier/docsOnly/state2/stackSkill/referenceApp skip Signature Accuracy + Type Coverage; naive mode coherence is already 0; null external validation means skip it
3. For each skipped category, set its weight to 0
4. Compute sum of active category weights
5. For each active category: `new_weight = old_weight / sum_active * 100`
6. `weighted_score = new_weight / 100 * category_score`
7. `total = sum of all weighted_scores`

Report: "**Note:** Scoring script unavailable — calculated manually per scoring-rules.md."

### 3d. Apply Post-Score Caps (BEFORE PASS/FAIL determination)

The scoring script returns `totalScore` and a preliminary `result`. Two caps may override the script's PASS into FAIL — applied in this order, and ONLY if the script did NOT return INCONCLUSIVE (floor always takes precedence):

**Cap 1 — Tooling degraded:** if `analysisConfidence == "degraded"` or `toolingStatus` indicates a missing helper (`python3-missing`, `frontmatter-validator-missing`), set `effective_score = min(totalScore, threshold - 1)` and record `scoring_notes: tooling degraded — capped below threshold until helper restored`.

**Cap 2 — Docs-only without external validators:** if `docsOnly == true` AND `externalValidation` score was null (neither skill-check nor tessl ran), set `effective_score = min(effective_score, threshold - 1)` and record `scoring_notes: docs-only without external validators — capped below threshold`.

If either cap fired and `result` was `PASS`, change it to `FAIL`. If `result` was already `INCONCLUSIVE`, leave it — the evidence-floor verdict is never overridden by a cap.

### 4. Determine Result (PASS / FAIL / INCONCLUSIVE)

The scoring script enforces the minimum-evidence floor BEFORE comparing score vs threshold. Three possible outcomes:

```
IF result == "INCONCLUSIVE" — minimum-evidence floor tripped; not PASS, not FAIL
IF score >= threshold       → PASS
IF score < threshold        → FAIL
```

**INCONCLUSIVE floor clauses** (see `{scoringRulesFile}`):
- `active_categories < 2` (after all redistribution), OR
- `tier == "Quick"` AND Export Coverage is the sole scoring contributor

**Tooling-degraded cap:** If the `analysisConfidence` in output frontmatter is `degraded` (python3 missing, frontmatter validator missing, or other degraded state flagged in step 1), the step MUST cap the score at `threshold - 1` BEFORE the PASS/FAIL comparison. This forces a deterministic FAIL until tooling is restored. Do NOT override an INCONCLUSIVE result with the cap — INCONCLUSIVE remains the verdict.

### 5. Determine Next Workflow Recommendation

Based on test result:

**IF PASS:**
- `nextWorkflow: 'export-skill'` — skill is ready for export
- **Drift override:** if workflow context carries
  `allow_workspace_drift: true` (set in step 1 §5b when the user passed
  `--allow-workspace-drift` AND the workspace HEAD did not match
  `metadata.source_commit`), the PASS is a **conditional PASS**:
  - Write `testResult: 'pass-with-drift'` to the output frontmatter instead of
    bare `'pass'`. The result contract (§4c of step 6) mirrors the same
    value.
  - Override `nextWorkflow` to `'update-skill'` — **refuse to recommend
    `export-skill`**. The drift override weakens the workflow's strongest
    false-positive guard (we tested against HEAD, not the pinned source); a
    PASS under drift is not trustworthy enough to promote to export without a
    clean re-test against the pinned commit.
  - Record `scoring_notes: workspace drift overridden — PASS is conditional; re-run against pinned commit before export`.

**IF FAIL:**
- `nextWorkflow: 'update-skill'` — skill needs remediation before export

**IF INCONCLUSIVE:**
- `nextWorkflow: 'manual-review'` — evidence base is insufficient to grade the skill automatically. The test report records `inconclusiveReasons` from the scoring script. Surface to the user — do not auto-recommend export or update.

### 6. Append Completeness Score to Output

Append the **Completeness Score** section to `{outputFile}`:

```markdown
## Completeness Score

### Score Breakdown

| Category | Score | Weight | Weighted |
|----------|-------|--------|----------|
| Export Coverage | {N}% | {W}% | {WS}% |
| Signature Accuracy | {N}% | {W}% | {WS}% |
| Type Coverage | {N}% | {W}% | {WS}% |
| Coherence | {N}% | {W}% | {WS}% |
| External Validation | {N}% | {W}% | {WS}% |
| **Total** | | **100%** | **{total}%** |

### Result

**Score:** {total}%
**Threshold:** {threshold}%
**Result:** **{PASS|FAIL|INCONCLUSIVE}**
{If INCONCLUSIVE:}
**Inconclusive Reasons:**
{bulleted list from script `inconclusiveReasons`}

**Threshold Source:** {threshold_source}
**Weight Distribution:** {naive (redistributed) | contextual (full)}
**Tier Adjustment:** {none | Quick tier — signature and type coverage not scored}
**External Validators:** {both available | skill-check only | tessl only | none — weight redistributed}
**Analysis Confidence:** {full | provenance-map | metadata-only | remote-only | docs-only}
```

If `analysis_confidence` is not `full`, append a degradation notice. **The notice must be confidence-aware** — see the degradation notice rules in `{sourceAccessProtocol}`:

```markdown
### Access Degradation Notice

**Resolved via:** {analysis_confidence} {confidence breakdown if provenance-map, e.g., "(T1 AST-verified at compilation time)" or "(12 T1, 3 T1-low)"}
**Impact:** {describe limitation — e.g., "Signature checks limited to name-matching. Source file:line citations from provenance-map, not live AST." — or "Provenance data is at highest confidence; no limitation." for all-T1 provenance-map}
**Recommendation:** {confidence-dependent — see {sourceAccessProtocol} degradation notice rules. Do NOT recommend local clone when provenance-map entries are already T1.}
```

### 7. Update Output Frontmatter

Update `{outputFile}` frontmatter:
- `testResult: '{pass|pass-with-drift|fail|inconclusive}'` (lowercase; mirrors script `result`, with `pass-with-drift` substituted for `pass` when `allow_workspace_drift` was set and drift was observed — see §5 drift override)
- `score: '{total}%'`
- `threshold: '{threshold}%'`
- `thresholdSource: '{threshold_source}'`
- `analysisConfidence: '{full|degraded|provenance-map|metadata-only|remote-only|docs-only}'`
- `nextWorkflow: '{export-skill|update-skill|manual-review}'`
- Append `'score'` to `stepsCompleted`

### 8. Report Score

"**Completeness score calculated.**

**{skill_name}:** **{total}%** — **{PASS|FAIL}**

| Category | Score | Weighted |
|----------|-------|----------|
| Export Coverage | {N}% | {WS}% |
| Signature Accuracy | {N}% | {WS}% |
| Type Coverage | {N}% | {WS}% |
| Coherence | {N}% | {WS}% |
| External Validation | {N}% | {WS}% |

**Threshold:** {threshold}%
**Recommendation:** {export-skill if pass | update-skill if fail}

**Proceeding to gap report...**"

Update stepsCompleted, then load and execute {nextStepFile}.

1	---
2	nextStepFile: 'health-check.md'
3	
4	outputFile: '{forge_version}/test-report-{skill_name}-{run_id}.md'
5	scoringRulesFile: 'references/scoring-rules.md'
6	outputFormatsFile: 'assets/output-section-formats.md'
7	# outputContractSchema and healthCheck resolve relative to the SKF module root

9	# development), NOT relative to this step file. Both paths are probed in

12	healthCheckProbeOrder:
13	  - '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/shared/health-check.md'
14	  - '{project-root}/src/shared/health-check.md'

18	---
19	
20	<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. Test report prose in {document_output_language}. -->
21	
22	# Step 6: Gap Report
23	
24	## STEP GOAL:
25	
26	Generate a detailed gap report listing every issue found during coverage and coherence analysis, assign severity to each gap, provide specific actionable remediation suggestions, and finalize the test report document. Do not recalculate scores — that ran in step 5. This step chains to the local health-check step via `{nextStepFile}` after completion; the user-facing report is NOT the terminal step.
27	
28	### 1. Collect All Issues
29	
30	Read `{outputFile}` and extract every issue found across all analysis sections:
31	
32	**From Coverage Analysis (step 03):**
33	- Missing documentation (exports in source but not in SKILL.md)
34	- Signature mismatches (documented signature differs from source)
35	- Stale documentation (documented but no longer in source)
36	- Type coverage gaps (undocumented types/interfaces)
37	
38	**From Coherence Analysis (step 04):**
39	- Broken references (file paths, skill references, type imports that don't resolve)
40	- Incomplete integration patterns (contextual mode)
41	- Structural issues (naive mode — missing sections, broken examples)
42	
43	**From External Validation (step 04b):**
44	- skill-check diagnostics (unresolved errors and warnings)
45	- tessl judge suggestions (content quality and actionability improvements)
46	
47	### 2. Load Severity Rules
48	
49	Load `{scoringRulesFile}` for gap severity classification:
50	
51	| Severity | Criteria |
52	|----------|----------|
53	| **Critical** | Missing exported function/class documentation |
54	| **High** | Signature mismatch between source and SKILL.md |
55	| **Medium** | Missing type or interface documentation |
56	| **Medium** | Migration section present/absent mismatch with T2-future annotation data (Deep tier) |
57	| **Medium** | Script/asset directory exists but no Scripts & Assets section in SKILL.md |
58	| **Medium** | Scripts & Assets section references file not found in scripts/ or assets/ directory |
59	| **Low** | Script/asset file present without provenance entry in provenance-map.json file_entries |
60	| **Low** | Missing optional metadata or examples |
61	| **Low** | Description trigger optimization recommended (third-person voice, negative triggers, or keyword coverage gaps) |
62	| **Info** | Style suggestions, non-blocking observations |
63	| **Info** | Discovery testing not performed — realistic prompt testing recommended before export |
64	
65	### 3. Classify and Order Gaps
66	
67	Load `{outputFormatsFile}` for gap entry format and remediation quality rules.
68	
69	For each issue, assign severity from `{scoringRulesFile}` and generate a specific remediation following the quality rules in `{outputFormatsFile}`. Remediation suggestions MUST reference specific files, exports, and line numbers. Order gaps by severity: Critical → High → Medium → Low → Info.
70	
71	### 4. Generate Remediation Summary and Append Gap Report
72	
73	Load the Gap Report section format from `{outputFormatsFile}`. Count gaps by severity, estimate effort per the guidelines in `{outputFormatsFile}`, and append the complete **Gap Report** section to `{outputFile}`.
74	
75	If no gaps found, append a clean pass message recommending **export-skill** workflow.
76	
77	### 4b. Discovery Testing (MANDATORY)
78	
79	**`--no-discovery` flag bypass (precedes the precondition check).** If `no_discovery: true` is set in workflow context (from §1 of `init.md` — `--no-discovery` flag on invocation), record an Info-severity note in the Discovery Quality subsection: `discovery — skipped: --no-discovery flag set`, log the bypass, and SKIP §4b.1–§4b.3. Proceed to §4b.4 (description optimization) only if tessl/skill-check flagged description issues; otherwise skip directly to §4c.
80	
81	After gap enumeration, perform minimum-viable discovery testing. This is a **Medium-weight** check contributing to the Discovery Quality subsection — no longer advisory boilerplate.
82	
83	**4b.0 Precondition — catalog size check:**
84	
85	Count the skills in `{skillsOutputFolder}`: `ls -1d {skillsOutputFolder}/*/ 2>/dev/null | wc -l` (directories only — each skill package lives at `{skillsOutputFolder}/<skill-name>/`).
86	
87	- If `catalog_size < 2`: **skip §4b.1–§4b.3**. Record an Info-severity note in the Discovery Quality subsection: `discovery — skipped: catalog size N={catalog_size}, requires ≥2 candidates for meaningful routing`. The routing test is vacuous with one candidate (any prompt returns the sole skill); reporting `3/3 PASS` under those conditions inflates the Discovery score and masks genuinely bad description triggers. Proceed to §4b.4 (description optimization) if tessl/skill-check flagged description issues; otherwise skip to §4c.
88	- If `catalog_size >= 2`: continue with §4b.1 as written.
89	
90	Optional escape hatch: the workflow accepts `--discovery-catalog=all` to broaden the candidate pool to `{project-root}/.claude/skills/` or `{project-root}/_bmad/agents/` for single-skill repos where the repo-local catalog is trivially too small. When the flag is set, rebuild `catalog_size` from the broader pool before the precondition check.
91	
92	**4b.1 Extract realistic prompts from the skill under test:**
93	
94	Parse SKILL.md for the three most "organic" prompts found in its `description`, `Triggers`, or example sections. Prefer prompts that:
95	- Use conversational phrasing (contractions, casual language, implicit context)
96	- Omit the skill name or explicit command invocation
97	- Reflect how a user would actually ask for this capability
98	
99	If SKILL.md does not contain enough organic examples, synthesize 3 from the skill's exports/capability summary using the patterns from §4b.4 below.
100	
101	**4b.2 Spawn a discovery subagent:**
102	
103	For each of the 3 prompts, spawn an isolated subagent with NO prior context about which skill is under test. Provide only:
104	1. A compact list of ALL skills available in `{skillsOutputFolder}` (name + description line from each skill's SKILL.md frontmatter)
105	2. The prompt text
106	
107	Instruction to the subagent:
108	
109	> "You are an agent selecting the best skill to handle a user request. Here is the catalog: {catalog}. The user says: '{prompt}'. Return JSON: `{\"selected_skill\": \"<name>\", \"confidence\": \"<high|medium|low>\", \"reasoning\": \"<one sentence>\"}`. If no skill fits, return `{\"selected_skill\": null, ...}`. Return ONLY JSON."
110	
111	**4b.3 Evaluate discovery results:**
112	
113	Parse the 3 responses. Schema-validate (required: `selected_skill`, `confidence`, `reasoning`). On any parse/schema failure, record the prompt as `discovery_result: error` and continue.
114	
115	For each prompt, PASS = `selected_skill == skill_name` (the skill under test), FAIL otherwise.
116	
117	- **3 of 3 PASS** → discovery check PASS (Info severity note, no gap)
118	- **2 of 3 PASS** → discovery check WARN → **Medium**-severity gap: `discovery — 1/3 realistic prompts misrouted`
119	- **≤1 of 3 PASS** → discovery check FAIL → **High**-severity gap: `discovery — {N}/3 realistic prompts misrouted; description triggers are not pulling the skill`
120	
121	Append the prompts, selected skills, and outcomes as a table in the Discovery Quality subsection.
122	
123	**4b.4 Description optimization (secondary):** If tessl `description_score` (from step 04b) is below 90%, or skill-check flagged description issues, add remediation hints to the Discovery Quality subsection:
124	- Third-person voice check
125	- Explicit trigger keywords matching real user phrasing
126	- Negative triggers ("NOT for: ...") to prevent false positives
127	- Alternative skill references for excluded use cases
128	
129	Realistic prompt patterns for synthesis (§4b.1 fallback):
130	- Vague: "can you help me with this {artifact} my boss sent"
131	- Implicit: "why did {metric} drop last {period}"
132	- Abbreviated: "run the {keyword} thing on this data"
133	
134	### 4c. Result Contract (atomic write)
135	
136	**Resolve `{atomicWriteHelper}`:** probe `{atomicWriteProbeOrder}`. HALT if neither candidate exists — the contract is a downstream-consumer protocol and must never be written non-atomically.
137	
138	Write the result contract per `{outputContractSchema}`:
139	- Per-run record: `{forge_version}/skf-test-skill-result-{run_id}.json` (the `{run_id}` set in step 1 §6a — already carries UTC timestamp + PID + random suffix, so no same-second collision).
140	- Latest copy: `{forge_version}/skf-test-skill-result-latest.json` (stable path for pipeline consumers — copy, not symlink).
141	
142	Both writes MUST go through the atomic writer so partial writes are never observable:
143	
144	```bash
145	# Build the JSON payload in memory, then:
146	cat payload.json | python3 {atomicWriteHelper} write --target {forge_version}/skf-test-skill-result-{run_id}.json
147	cat payload.json | python3 {atomicWriteHelper} write --target {forge_version}/skf-test-skill-result-latest.json
148	```
149	
150	Payload contents:
151	- `outputs[]` — include the test report path at `{outputFile}` with its `{run_id}` suffix
152	- `summary` — `score`, `threshold`, `result` (`"PASS"`, `"PASS_WITH_DRIFT"`, `"FAIL"`, or **`"INCONCLUSIVE"`**), `testMode` (naive/contextual), `activeCategories[]`, `inconclusiveReasons[]` (when present). `PASS_WITH_DRIFT` is set when the workflow observed workspace drift and the user passed `--allow-workspace-drift` — see step 5 §5 drift override. Downstream consumers MUST treat `PASS_WITH_DRIFT` as a non-exportable result: re-run against the pinned commit before export.
153	- `runId` — the workflow's `{run_id}` for downstream correlation
154	- `healthCheckDispatched` — boolean, set by §7 after the dispatch decision
155	
156	The `{forge_version}/.test-skill.lock` acquired in step 1 §6b remains held until the end of this step — it guards against concurrent latest-file overwrites.
157	
158	**Post-finalization hook.** If `{onCompleteCommand}` (resolved in SKILL.md On Activation §3 from `workflow.on_complete` scalar) is non-empty, invoke it as:
159	
160	```bash
161	{onCompleteCommand} --result-path={forge_version}/skf-test-skill-result-{run_id}.json
162	```
163	
164	Run it with a bounded timeout (default 60s). On success: log Info note "on_complete — invoked: {command}" and continue. On non-zero exit, timeout, or any failure: append the failure reason to `workflow_warnings[]` (e.g. `on_complete — failed (exit {N}): {stderr_first_line}`) and continue. **The hook must never fail the workflow** — its purpose is integration glue (notify a CI router, post to a queue, archive the result) and any failure there is orthogonal to the test verdict. If `{onCompleteCommand}` is empty, this hook is a no-op (no log entry needed).
165	
166	### 5. Finalize Output Document — Enforce Step Completeness
167	
168	**Incremental step tracking:** read `stepsCompleted` from the output frontmatter. The expected set is the canonical chain:
169	
170	```
171	['init',
172	 'detect-mode',
173	 'coverage-check',
174	 'coherence-check',
175	 'external-validators',
176	 'hard-gate',
177	 'score',
178	 'report']
179	```
180	
181	If any expected entry is missing, HALT with "step completeness violation — missing {list}; workflow state is inconsistent, do not finalize the report". Only append `'report'` and write back after the check passes.
182	
183	**Section anchor presence check (companion to stepsCompleted).** The
184	report template ships six canonical H2 anchors — one per populating step. An
185	off-sequence run (e.g. a step wrote its section into the wrong anchor, or a
186	subagent truncated the file) can leave `stepsCompleted` intact while a section
187	is missing. `grep -n` each anchor below against `{outputFile}`; each MUST
188	return ≥1 match:
189	
190	```
191	^## Test Summary$
192	^## Coverage Analysis$
193	^## Coherence Analysis$
194	^## External Validation$
195	^## Completeness Score$
196	^## Gap Report$
197	```
198	
199	On any miss, HALT with "report anchor missing: {anchor} — section was not
200	appended by its owning step". Do NOT append `'report'` and do NOT
201	write the result contract. The template in `templates/test-report-template.md`
202	declares these anchors as TBD placeholders; a miss means a step silently
203	skipped its append.
204	
205	**INCONCLUSIVE as gate:** if `testResult == 'inconclusive'` (from step 5), the report final presentation (§6) and result contract (§4c) have already been written with that verdict. Do NOT auto-map INCONCLUSIVE to PASS or FAIL. Recommend `manual-review`. The step must still complete (health-check runs unconditionally) — INCONCLUSIVE is a report-time signal, not a workflow abort.
206	
207	### 6. Present Final Report
208	
209	"**Test complete for {skill_name}.**
210	
211	---
212	
213	**Result:** **{PASS|PASS_WITH_DRIFT|FAIL|INCONCLUSIVE}** — **{score}%** (threshold: {threshold}%)
214	
215	**Gaps Found:** {total_gaps}
216	- Critical: {N}
217	- High: {N}
218	- Medium: {N}
219	- Low: {N}
220	- Info: {N}
221	
222	**Report saved to:** `{outputFile}`
223	
224	---
225	
226	**Recommended next step:**
227	
228	{IF PASS:}
229	**export-skill** — This skill is ready for export. Run the export-skill workflow to package it for distribution.
230	
231	{IF PASS_WITH_DRIFT:}
232	**update-skill** — The skill scored above threshold, but `--allow-workspace-drift` was in effect: the test ran against workspace HEAD, not `metadata.source_commit`. A conditional PASS is not trustworthy enough to export. Re-sync the source tree to the pinned commit (or re-extract against current HEAD) and re-run test-skill without the drift override before exporting.
233	
234	{IF FAIL:}
235	**update-skill** — This skill needs remediation. Review the gap report above and run the update-skill workflow to address the {N} blocking issues (Critical + High).
236	
237	{IF INCONCLUSIVE:}
238	**manual-review** — The evidence base was too thin to grade automatically. See `inconclusiveReasons` in the Completeness Score section. Typical fixes: upgrade forge tier, enable external validators, or re-extract with a wider scope. Do NOT export.
239	
240	---
241	
242	**See Discovery Quality section in the report for description optimization and realistic prompt testing recommendations.**
243	
244	**Test report finalized.**"
245	
246	### 6b. Headless Exit Status
247	
248	If `{headless_mode}`:
249	- `testResult: 'pass'` → `exit 0`
250	- `testResult: 'pass-with-drift'` → `exit 4` (distinct from clean pass — orchestrators MUST route to re-test-against-pinned-commit queues and refuse export; never exit 0 under drift override)
251	- `testResult: 'fail'` → `exit 2` (after the result contract has been written in §4c — never before)
252	- `testResult: 'inconclusive'` → `exit 3` (distinct from fail so orchestrators can route to manual-review queues)
253	
254	Non-headless mode always drops to the menu in §7.
255	
256	### 7. Health-Check Dispatch + MENU OPTIONS
257	
258	**`--no-health-check` flag bypass (precedes the health-check resolution).** If `no_health_check: true` is set in workflow context (from §1 of `init.md` — `--no-health-check` flag on invocation), set `health_check_dispatched: false` in the output report frontmatter and mirror `healthCheckDispatched: false` into the result contract written in §4c (re-write atomically via `{atomicWriteHelper}`). Log Info note "health-check — skipped: --no-health-check flag set" and EXIT THE WORKFLOW: in `{headless_mode}`, exit with the code already determined in §6b; non-headless, simply terminate after the §6 presentation. Do NOT resolve `{healthCheckFile}`, do NOT display the menu, do NOT chain to `{nextStepFile}`. The §6b exit branch is the terminal state under this flag.
259	
260	Resolve `{healthCheckFile}`: probe `{healthCheckProbeOrder}` in order. **HALT** if neither candidate exists — the health-check is the true terminal step; without it the workflow cannot complete honestly:
261	
262	```
263	Error: cannot locate shared/health-check.md at either of:
264	  - {project-root}/_bmad/skf/shared/health-check.md
265	  - {project-root}/src/shared/health-check.md
266	
267	test-skill delegates its terminal step to the shared health-check. Install
268	the SKF module or run from a development checkout with src/ present.
269	```
270	
271	Before displaying the menu, write the dispatch decision into the output report frontmatter (so the artifact records whether the health-check ran):
272	
273	- `health_check_dispatched: true` — when the C menu choice will be taken (headless, or user will select C)
274	- `health_check_dispatched: false` — should be rare (only if operator explicitly skips, e.g. future flag)
275	
276	Also mirror the boolean into the `healthCheckDispatched` field of the result contract written in §4c (re-write atomically via `{atomicWriteHelper}` if the dispatch decision is made after the initial contract write).
277	
278	Display: "**Test complete.** [C] Finish"
279	
280	On [C] (or auto-proceed in `{headless_mode}` — log: "headless: auto-continue past report menu"): set `health_check_dispatched: true` in frontmatter, then load and execute `{nextStepFile}` (the local health-check dispatcher). The test report document at `{outputFile}` contains the full analysis: Test Summary, Coverage Analysis, Coherence Analysis, Completeness Score, and Gap Report.
281	
282
